steps: Identify all parties and their roles: controller, processor, or sub-processor
Check for a lawful basis clause (consent, legitimate interest, contractual necessity, etc.)
Verify data subject rights are addressed: access, erasure, portability, objection
Check for data retention limits and deletion obligations
Identify international transfer mechanisms (SCCs, adequacy decision, BCRs)
Flag any clauses that transfer GDPR liability without corresponding controls
Confirm a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) is present if one party processes for the other
Summarise gaps as a numbered list with article references
constraints: cite GDPR article numbers for each finding
distinguish critical gaps from advisory findings
do not give legal advice — flag for qualified review
failure_modes: missing sub-processor clauses that create downstream liability
confusing data controller with data processor obligations
overlooking implicit transfers via cloud provider data centres
treating boilerplate GDPR language as compliant without verifying substance
